---
title: "Expectation, Variance, Covariance"
---

## Role

This page explains the core summary statistics that almost every probabilistic argument uses.

## Intuition

- `expectation` is the average or center of mass
- `variance` measures spread around that center
- `covariance` measures how two random variables move together

## Formal Core

For a random variable $X$,

$$
\mathbb{E}[X]
$$

is its expectation, and

$$
\mathrm{Var}(X) = \mathbb{E}\big[(X-\mathbb{E}[X])^2\big]
$$

is its variance.

For two random variables $X$ and $Y$,

$$
\mathrm{Cov}(X,Y)=\mathbb{E}\big[(X-\mathbb{E}[X])(Y-\mathbb{E}[Y])\big].
$$

## Why It Matters

These quantities power:

- risk calculations
- estimation error analysis
- concentration bounds
- Gaussian models
- covariance matrix methods

## Application Lens

In data analysis and ML, covariance is often the hidden object underneath PCA, regression geometry, Gaussian modeling, and uncertainty quantification.

## Common Mistakes

- confusing variance with standard deviation
- assuming zero covariance means full independence
- forgetting what distribution expectation is taken over
